status_label: Preview tokenomics — not a public mainnet token
mint_policy_label: Fixed hard cap — no unlimited mint

active_capacity_model.headline: Active Capacity Reservation — not a fixed-term lease
principles: ACAP belongs to the holder. There is no fixed expiration term.
You reserve active network capacity — you are not renting tokens for a fixed period.
ACAP remains yours while held; nothing expires or is taken back after a term.
Active-capacity status depends on movement, node use, reservation activity or other network participation.
Inactive ACAP is not confiscated, but its active-capacity weight may pause, decay or become inactive until reactivated.
Reactivation can happen through movement, node participation or other defined network actions.
Value comes from capacity, movement, participation, anti-spam and network usefulness — not from a rental clock.

allocations.label: Public / community / early users
allocations.notes: Distributed via planned reservation pools and community programs.

allocations.label: Node operator reserve
allocations.notes: Reserved for node operators. Released by transparent rule. Not live.

allocations.label: Ecosystem grants / developers / integrations
allocations.notes: Grants for builders and integrations; rule-based release.

allocations.label: Liquidity / exchange reserve
allocations.notes: Reserved for future liquidity. No trading or listing is live.

allocations.label: Treasury / company reserve
allocations.notes: Long-term operations and runway; transparent release.

allocations.label: Team / founders (locked)
allocations.notes: Vesting/lock schedule defined before any release.

allocations.label: Strategic partners / advisors / legal / infrastructure
allocations.notes: Partnerships, legal and infrastructure; rule-based release.

current_pool.name: Active Capacity Reservation Pool 1
current_pool.note: Planned reservation pool. Not open. The next reservation tier becomes more expensive as the active-capacity pool is reserved. This is a capacity-pricing mechanism, not an investment promise.

rounds.notes: Planned earliest reservation round (Pool 1), tiered pricing. Not open. Subject to final legal and contract review.
Draft. Price/tier to be defined. Not open.
Draft. Price/tier to be defined. Not open.

utility.label: Node participation
utility.text: Participation weight for running a node in the network (design stage).

utility.label: Active capacity reservation
utility.text: Reserve active network capacity for projects and issuers — no fixed term.

utility.label: Anti-spam / network actions
utility.text: Small cost on network actions to deter spam and abuse.

utility.label: Future validator / operator reputation
utility.text: Planned input into validator and operator reputation scoring.

utility.label: Ecosystem access
utility.text: Access to ecosystem features, grants and integrations.

utility.label: Possible future fee / payment unit
utility.text: Possible future unit of account for fees inside the network. Not a payment system today.

divisibility.note: ACAP is planned as a divisible token. The technical token can use 18 decimals; user-facing UI may display 2-4 decimals for readability.

minting.reserve_release_rule: Any reserve release must be transparent and documented in the changelog.
minting.future_controls: Future contract changes must require multisig / timelock / governance when implemented.

disclaimers: ACAP tokenomics are draft / preview and may change before final launch.
No public mainnet token sale is active. No reservation is open.
This page is not investment advice. Tiered reservation pricing is a capacity mechanism, not a profit promise.
ACAP is presented as a utility / capacity token design.
Active Capacity Reservation is not a fixed-term lease: ACAP remains yours while held; nothing expires or is confiscated.
No custody, trading, bridge, payments, public rewards or public mainnet are live.
Numbers can change before final launch and will be versioned in the changelog.
No on-chain contract is deployed; no contract address is published.

changelog.change: Reworked to the Active Capacity Reservation model (owner rejected fixed-term lease wording): no fixed term, ACAP remains the holder's, active-capacity status depends on movement/node/participation, inactive ACAP not confiscated. Added Pool 1 (1,000,000 ACAP, tiered $0.001-$0.003), pricing_tiers, active_capacity_model. Rounds: 1M planned / 4M draft / 20M draft. reservation_active=false, available_for_reservation=0.
Initial preview tokenomics registry: 1,000,000,000 ACAP hard cap, 18 decimals, 7-way allocation draft. Sale disabled, circulating 0, available 0.
